content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
December 18, 1997
Phillip Yarbrough
178-2 Singingwood
Orange, California 92869
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-560
Dear Mr. Yarbrough:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions ofthe
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTIONS
1) For purposes of Section 85305 and the fundraising blackout period, how do you
calculate the number ofresidents in your district to determine if there are more than 1 million
residents?
2) Are blackout periods limited to financial contributions? Specifically, may you solicit
other officeholders to endorse your campaign?
CONCLUSIONS
1) The Commission has neither the authority nor the ability to determine the number of
residents in a district. Since the Commission does not make that determination, it has advised
that a local jurisdiction may use the most recent census or other reliable data to determine the
number ofresidents in a district. Any reasonable method of determining this number is
acceptable.
2) During the blackout period, you may solicit other officeholders to endorse your
campaign, but they may not make any monetary or in-kind contributions.
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 450, Sacramento, California 95814 (916) 322-5660


File No. A-97-560
Page 2
FACTS
You are a Trustee for the Rancho Santiago Community College District (“district”) and
face reelection in November 1998. The jurisdiction of the district encompasses two full cities,
portions of six other cities and approximately one half of the Cleveland National Forest.
You would like to solicit other officeholders to endorse your campaign. The
officeholders would lend their name and support to you, but would not make any monetary or
in-kind contributions.
ANALYSIS
Question 1
Section 85305 (copy enclosed) establishes restrictions on when a candidate may receive
contributions. In districts of less than 1,000,000 residents, candidates may accept contributions
six months before the primary or regular election. In districts of 1,000,000 residents or more,
candidates may accept contributions 12 months before the primary or regular election.
The Fair Political Practices Commission (“Commission”) does not make the
determination ofhow many residents are in a particular district. The Commission has advised
that a local jurisdiction may use the most recently available census data or other reliable
information to determine the number of residents in a district. Any reasonable method of
determining this number is acceptable. (Hawthorn Advice Letter, No. A-97-316; Dowden
Advice Letter, No. A-97-295; Cosgrove Advice Letter, No. 1-97-109, copies enclosed.)
We have advised that where one candidate is elected separately from each division of a
water district, the term “district” is applied to each constituent division of the water district rather
than to the jurisdictional boundary ofthe entire water district. (Cosgrove, supra; Vilardi Advice
Letter, No. A-97-086, copy enclosed.)
Question 2
During the fundraising blackout period, candidates are prohibited from accepting any
contributions for an upcoming election, including in-kind contributions. (Section 85305.) A
candidate may solicit endorsements prior to the date on which he or she may begin fundraising
for an upcoming election. However, depending on how the money is spent and by whom,
subsequent expenditures made to announce or publicize an endorsement may constitute a
contribution to the candidate in violation of the fundraising blackout periods. (Edmonds Advice
Letter, No. A-97-137, copy enclosed.)


File No. A-97-560
Page 3
Therefore, you may solicit other officeholders to endorse your campaign, but they may
not make any monetary or in-kind contributions during the fundraising blackout period.
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Ji)Stec er
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:JS :tls
Enclosures
